vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Provide structural support for the wing spar
Mount the motor pylon securely
Allow access to the equipment bay door
Dissipate heat from the motor pod
Ensure proper thermal management
Maintain structural integrity under load
Facilitate easy service access

verify.rule: The blueprint must include all specified fields.
The mass estimate must not exceed the budgeted weight.

verify.value: Each feature in the part_tree references a specific geometry_op.
verify.rule: Features must map directly to geometry operations.

verify.value: Interfaces are clearly defined and reciprocal between subsystems.
verify.rule: Interfaces must be clearly defined and reciprocal.

verify.value: Visible locking methods and preload evidence are provided for critical joints.
verify.rule: Critical joints must show locking methods and preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex interface geometry.
risk.mit: Implement detailed assembly instructions and visual aids.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling performance if cooling inlet is obstructed.
risk.mit: Include clearances and access points for regular maintenance.

risk.desc: Weak interface at the motor pylon mount could lead to structural failure.
risk.mit: Conduct stress tests and reinforce if necessary.

risk.desc: Tolerance mismatch between FDM and foam core materials may affect fit.
risk.mit: Review and adjust manufacturing tolerances accordingly.

risk.desc: Blocked access for maintenance and inspection due to tight integration.
risk.mit: Design with sufficient access windows and clear labeling.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Equipment Bay Door, Wing Spar Reinforcement, Motor Pylon Mount

physics_paths.description: Heat transfer from motor pod to cooling inlet for dissipation.
Structural load transfer from wing spar to reinforcement rib.
Access for maintenance and equipment installation/removal.

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate avionics sensors within the equipment bay door area.
Route data cables through the cooling inlet channel.
Establish a fluid path for cooling purposes.

manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures oriented for minimal interference.
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit.

verification_focus.description: Verify the mass of the center body pod does not exceed the budgeted 1500g.
Test the spar reinforcement rib's ability to withstand expected loads.
Ensure the cooling inlet effectively dissipates heat from the motor pod.

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structural integrity under aerodynamic and payload loads
Continuous load path from wing skin to fuselage frames
Anti-rotation functionality
Service access for battery and payload modules
Thermal management through CFRP spar

verify.rule: Ensure all required evidence features are present.
Ensure the estimated mass matches the design brief.
Ensure all parts and features trace back to the initial design.
Ensure interfaces are reciprocal between adjacent modules.
Ensure critical joints have visible locking/preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex geometry.
risk.mit: Implement detailed assembly instructions and visual aids.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion affecting structural integrity.
risk.mit: Use materials with similar thermal expansion coefficients.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance in bolted connections may cause binding.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearances to ensure proper fit.

risk.desc: Potential for misidentification of fasteners during assembly.
risk.mit: Label all fasteners and provide a parts list.

risk.desc: Risk of improper torque application leading to joint failure.
risk.mit: Train assembly personnel on proper torque application techniques.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.description: Transfers lift, landing, and payload loads from wing skin to fuselage frames.
Heat dissipation from sun exposure through CFRP spar.
Resists rotational forces around the wing's longitudinal axis.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Support structures oriented for easy removal
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit, CMM for critical dimensions

check_items: Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure
Key prevents unwanted rotation of the spar
All bolted connections meet specified torque requirements

generated_parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
generated_parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

requirements: Provide structural support for the wing skin
Facilitate airflow over the wing for lift generation
Include control surface cutouts and hinges
Integrate with the wing spar and skin
Provide access for sensors and serviceability
Maintain structural integrity under specified loads

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.

verify.value: Interfaces with Wing-Spar, Wing-Skin, Control-Surface-Hinge are named and reciprocal.
verify.rule: Interface rule: include at least three adjacent-module interfaces or mounting datums and at least three negative/service features such as holes, pockets, ducts, ports, or access cuts.

verify.value: Visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence for critical joints.
verify.rule: Joint evidence rule: critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to snap-fit tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances and visual inspection steps.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure if load path continuity is compromised.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis and ensure continuous structural elements.

risk.desc: Improper fastener locking could lead to loosening over time.
risk.mit: Specify proper torque values and conduct periodic inspections.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing planform, airfoil section evidence, winglets, elevon/control surface cutouts with hinges

physics_paths.description: Lift force transfers from wing skin through ribs to spar
Airflow over wing rib contributing to lift
Heat dissipation from electronics within rib

manufacturing_strategy.support: orientation: vertical, inspection: visual and CMM

verification_focus.description: Ensure total mass does not exceed 150g

risk.mit: Implement additional alignment features and provide clear assembly instructions.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Servo pockets, control horns, pushrod/linkage channels
physics_paths: Load path: MG90S or DS215MG servo load transferred to wing structure via servo pocket
Flow path: Control horn linkage movement from servo to elevon
Thermal path: Heat dissipation from servo to surrounding wing structure
integration_opportunities: Optimize servo mounting pattern for weight reduction while maintaining structural integrity
Improve thermal dissipation fins for better heat transfer
Enhance control horn linkage channel for smoother operation

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit of components

verification_focus: Mass verification: Ensure total mass does not exceed 100g
Alignment verification: Confirm correct positioning of servo mounting pattern and control horn linkage channel
Structural integrity verification: Validate the strength of the servo pocket under expected loads

risk.desc: Potential interference between telemetry board and antenna channel due to close proximity.
risk.mit: Increase spacing between telemetry board and antenna channel to ensure clear air flow and signal integrity.

risk.desc: Limited access to service access point may hinder maintenance operations.
risk.mit: Ensure that the service access point is easily accessible without disassembling the main structure.

risk.desc: Strain relief may not be sufficient if cables are routed through sharp bends.
risk.mit: Implement additional strain relief features at sharp bends to protect cables.

risk.desc: GPS signal may be affected by nearby electronic components.
risk.mit: Ensure proper signal separation and shielding between GPS antenna and other electronic components.

risk.desc: Pitot boom may be susceptible to damage during assembly or operation.
risk.mit: Add protective measures around the pitot boom to prevent damage during handling.

Telemetry data from board to antenna
